mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions through lower discount rates and higher risk appetite, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate is falling from already low levels and fails if rate cuts coincide with recessionary labor deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03 despite persistent policy easing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market stress, weaker household income growth, and lower equity earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should appear only when unemployment is clearly accelerating, not during stable high-unemployment plateaus.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the effect flips positive in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An interaction between rising RSAFS and improving PAYEMS indicates broadening consumer demand and payroll-supported sales growth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism depends on sales expansion being matched by labor-market absorption; sales growth alone without payroll gains is not sufficient.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with concurrent RSAFS and PAYEMS improvement do not outperform baseline 60-day returns, or if either series alone explains the effect without the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after a prior contraction signals cyclical industrial recovery, tighter inventory liquidation ending, and better cyclical earnings momentum, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is valid only when industrial production is moving up from a weak base.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% month-over-month after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if rebound episodes are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the signal vanishes after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A housing start surge in HOUST above trend signals stronger construction activity, wealth effects, and cyclical demand acceleration, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect should weaken when housing is rising only because of policy distortions without corroborating demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if high-HOUST regimes fail to outperform over 60 trading days, or if the predictive sign is negative in a majority of rolling 2-year subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A rising CPIAUCSL combined with a still-low FEDFUNDS rate implies negative real policy rates and easier real financial conditions, which supports equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails if inflation rises while the policy rate also rises quickly enough to neutralize the real-rate impulse.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of higher CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS does not improve 20-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS is included alone.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating PAYEMS trend indicates payroll growth, stronger income formation, and better forward revenue visibility for cyclically exposed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days. The signal is a short-horizon confirmation of labor momentum and should be strongest when payroll gains are broad and sustained.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the effect is not distinguishable from noise in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is weak while UNRATE is rising, the economy is entering a demand-slowdown regime that reduces earnings expectations and raises recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The joint condition is necessary because weak GDP alone without labor deterioration does not fully capture recession probability.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weak GDPC1 and rising UNRATE episodes do not underperform over 60 trading days, or if the predictive sign becomes positive in any sustained rolling 3-year window.
